what_it_does: Runs a Node.js lint proof over dna/types.json and live registry files, validating edge law metadata, declared relation kinds and endpoints, predicate target resolution, registry file coverage, and registry type coverage before exiting with a pass/fail status.
observations: Loads dna/types.json once and lazily caches parsed face JSON and arbitrary file JSON.
Derives project paths from the module URL, with root two levels above engine/lint.
Implements a small locator language for dotted paths, dictionary wildcards, array item expansion, and key extraction.
Predicate checks compare located source values against target sets built from disk, code exports, or located registry data.
The code target reader extracts names from a DNA.org object literal using a regular expression.
The registry/disk check validates both dna/*.json declarations and declared non-dna files.
Console output is the only reporting channel, and process exit status reflects whether any failures were recorded.
standouts: The lint logic is declaration-driven from dna/types.json rather than hard-coding specific registries.
The locator and targetSet helpers form the file-local substrate for resolving references across JSON faces, disk files, and code symbols.
The script treats relationship metadata as bidirectional verb/opposite declarations and validates those declarations before reference checks.
conventions: Uses ESM imports from Node built-ins.
Uses top-level script execution rather than exported functions.
Uses lazy memoization maps for repeated JSON reads.
Uses underscore-prefixed keys as metadata to skip during registry and locator enumeration.
Reports checks with compact pass/fail console glyphs and exits nonzero on failure.
concerns: The section 2 success message depends on global fail being zero, so an earlier failure can suppress a local success report.
targetSet returns every dna JSON file for any disk target without using t.disk as a selector.
The code target parser depends on a narrow DNA.org assignment format and may not recognize equivalent JavaScript shapes.
JSON parsing and file reads are not guarded, so malformed or missing required inputs terminate the script outside the bad() reporting path.
notes: This file is a declaration-driven lint proof that checks whether typed relationship declarations and live registry artifacts agree.
questions: Should disk targets ever be scoped by t.disk rather than all dna JSON files?
Is the DNA.org regex intended to be the complete code-symbol contract?
Should section success messages be independent of failures from earlier sections?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it validates the declared type-system edges, registry coverage, and live predicate references from dna/types.json against files on disk.
apparent_themes: Small local query language over JSON structures
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make dna/types.json an executable contract so newly declared registries, relation kinds, edges, and type coverage are checked immediately against the repository contents.
novelty: It combines schema-like declaration checks with live cross-face reference resolution and a compact custom locator syntax in a single lint script.
contribution: This file contributes an executable validation layer for declared registries, relation edges, predicate references, disk-facing registry coverage, and registry type coverage, preserving the contract that design/code substrates must name their relationships and resolve against live artifacts.
summary_for_embedding: engine/lint/types.mjs is a Node.js lint proof that reads dna/types.json and repository registry files to validate edge metadata, relation declarations, predicate references, registry disk coverage, and primary type coverage.
intention_for_embedding: It was for turning the declared type-system ledger into an executable consistency check over live design/code substrate files.

symbols.does: Records and prints a passing check message.
symbols.description: Increments the pass counter and writes a formatted success line to stdout. It is the positive reporting primitive used by each lint section.

symbols.does: Records and prints a failing check message.
symbols.description: Increments the fail counter and writes a formatted failure line to stdout. It is the failure reporting primitive that also determines the final process exit status through the shared fail counter.

symbols.does: Loads and memoizes a dna face JSON file by name.
symbols.description: Reads dna/<name>.json relative to the project root, parses it as JSON, and caches the result in the faces map. It provides named access to declared face files.

symbols.does: Loads and memoizes a JSON file by repository-relative path.
symbols.description: Reads a file path relative to the project root, parses it as JSON, and caches it in the files map. It supports locators that point outside the standard dna face naming convention.

symbols.does: Selects a source object from either a file locator or face locator.
symbols.description: Returns byFile(c.file) when a locator names a file, otherwise returns face(c.face). It is the normalization point for source and target locator inputs used by locate().

symbols.does: Evaluates the file's dotted locator syntax against an object.
symbols.description: Walks an object through dot-separated steps, supporting wildcard dictionary values with '*', key extraction with '~keys', and array expansion with '[]'. It filters undefined values at the end and skips underscore-prefixed keys where the syntax enumerates object entries.

symbols.does: Builds a set of valid target strings for a predicate check.
symbols.description: Creates target identifiers from disk dna files, exported DNA.org generator names in a code file, or located values inside a JSON source. Object values are reduced to their id field, and only string values are retained in the returned Set.
